entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Author of the Hiring Signal Collapse position; founder of Careerspan

entities.name: Hiring Signal Collapse
entities.context: Position that inbound hiring is structurally broken because AI eliminated the signal value of personalization

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company operating in the hiring/career domain

entities.name: AI Personalization
entities.context: AI's ability to generate plausible tailored content at zero cost, destroying signal value

entities.name: Signal Theory
entities.context: Framework: when cost of producing a signal drops to zero, the signal loses informational value

entities.name: Self-Knowledge
entities.context: The deeper problem beneath personalization — people lack sufficient self-awareness to personalize meaningfully

entities.name: Inbound Hiring
entities.context: The traditional mechanism of candidates applying to jobs, now structurally broken per this position

entities.name: Detection Problem
entities.context: Recruiters cannot distinguish genuine effort from AI-generated content in applications

relationships.context: This is a formal position in V's knowledge system under hiring-market domain
Founded Careerspan, which operates in the hiring domain this position addresses
AI making personalization free is the proximate cause of signal collapse
Position is grounded in signal economics — zero-cost signals carry no information
The real root cause: people lack self-knowledge, and AI lets them bypass developing it
Inbound hiring is the system declared structurally broken by this position
AI-generated content is indistinguishable from genuine effort, making detection impossible
AI personalization masks the absence of self-knowledge rather than building it
Careerspan likely addresses the broken hiring signal problem V has identified
